name: Sendov's Theorem

phase_0.boundary_condition: the closed disk containing all roots of a polynomial and its derivative

VINIT.justification: void state before any polynomial is given; the initial uninitialized object awaiting definition

TANCH.chosen_element: the Sendov disk: |z| ≤ 1 + max|roots of P|
TANCH.justification: terminal anchor that closes and contains the entire system; the hermetic boundary within which all critical points must lie

AFWD.justification: forward morphism: the directed transformation from polynomial to its derivative, ascending from base form to derived form

AREV.chosen_element: root recovery: critical points → original roots
AREV.justification: reverse/descent morphism: the contravariant path from critical points back toward the root structure; descent from derivative to base

CLINK.chosen_element: composition of root-finding and critical-point-finding operations
CLINK.justification: sequential chaining: find roots of P, then find critical points of P', then verify containment; each step feeds the next

IMSCRIB.justification: self-reference; the polynomial recognizes itself as the object under examination; the stone that knows itself

FSPLIT.chosen_element: partition of critical points into two classes: those inside the Sendov disk vs. those outside
FSPLIT.justification: co-multiplication δ: one set of critical points branches into two distinct subsets based on containment criterion |c - r| ≤ 1 for some root r

FFUSE.chosen_element: reconstitution of critical points: both inside and outside classes reunite into the full set of critical points of P'
FFUSE.justification: multiplication μ: the two branches (inside/outside) fuse back into the complete derivative structure; FFUSE(FSPLIT(critical_points)) = critical_points

EVALT.chosen_element: affirmative case: all critical points lie within the Sendov disk
EVALT.justification: true/success branch; Sendov's conjecture holds for this polynomial; positive evaluation

EVALF.chosen_element: negative case: at least one critical point lies outside the Sendov disk
EVALF.justification: false/failure branch; counterexample to Sendov's conjecture; negative evaluation

ENGAGR.chosen_element: the boundary case: a critical point lies exactly on the Sendov disk boundary
ENGAGR.justification: paradice held without resolution; both inside and outside simultaneously; the albedo stage where the conjecture is neither proven nor disproven for this point

IFIX.chosen_element: permanent record of verification: the polynomial P and its critical points are logged as verified or counterexample
IFIX.justification: ROM fixation; append-only record; once a polynomial is tested, the result cannot be undone; the measurement record of mathematical truth

phase_2.split_element: FSPLIT: partition critical points into {inside Sendov disk} and {outside Sendov disk}
phase_2.split_input: the complete set of critical points of P'(z)
split_outputs: critical points c where ∃ root r: |c - r| ≤ 1
critical points c where ∀ roots r: |c - r| > 1
phase_2.fuse_element: FFUSE: reconstitute the full set of critical points from both branches
phase_2.fuse_result: the complete set of critical points of P'(z) — exactly the input to FSPLIT

phase_3.void_description: 00 — no polynomial specified; no roots, no critical points, no disk defined; the uninitialized state before Sendov's theorem is invoked
phase_3.true_description: 01 — Sendov's conjecture verified for this polynomial: every critical point lies within the disk |z| ≤ 1 + max|roots|; affirmative state
phase_3.false_description: 10 — counterexample found: at least one critical point lies outside the Sendov disk; negative state; conjecture fails for this instance
phase_3.both_description: 11 — paradice: a critical point lies exactly on the boundary of the Sendov disk; simultaneously inside and outside; unresolved state

steps.domain_action: uninitialized polynomial P(z) of degree n ≥ 1; void state before any structure is imposed
establish the Sendov disk boundary: |z| ≤ 1 + max|r₁, r₂, ..., rₙ| where rᵢ are roots of P
compute the derivative P'(z); forward morphism from polynomial to its derived form
chain the operations: (1) find all roots r₁, ..., rₙ of P, (2) find all critical points c₁, ..., cₙ₋₁ of P'
verify identity: the polynomial P is the object under examination; self-recognition
partition critical points into two branches: T-branch = {c : ∃r, |c - r| ≤ 1}, F-branch = {c : ∀r, |c - r| > 1}
T-branch evaluation: for each critical point in T-branch, affirm that it satisfies the Sendov condition
F-branch evaluation: for each critical point in F-branch, negate the Sendov condition; these are potential counterexamples
boundary case: if any critical point c satisfies |c - r| = 1 for some root r, hold both EVALT and EVALF simultaneously; paradice state
reconstitute the full set of critical points from both branches; verify that FFUSE(FSPLIT(critical_points)) = critical_points
reverse morphism: trace critical points back to their relationship with roots; descent from derivative structure to root structure
compose the verification: (1) check all critical points against all roots, (2) verify containment in Sendov disk, (3) aggregate results
permanent record: log the polynomial P, its roots, its critical points, and the verification result (PASS/FAIL/BOUNDARY); append-only, irreversible

phase_5.compiler_frontend: polynomial parser and algebraic solver: translates the domain intention 'verify Sendov's conjecture for P' into root-finding, differentiation, and distance-checking operations
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: algebraic communication: roots communicate their positions to critical points via the distance metric |c - r|; critical points report their containment status to the Sendov disk boundary
phase_5.memory_mechanism: root store and critical-point register: roots {r₁, ..., rₙ} and critical points {c₁, ..., cₙ₋₁} are stored; the Sendov disk radius is cached; verification results are appended to the log
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: verification cycle: (1) compute roots, (2) compute derivative, (3) compute critical points, (4) check each critical point against each root, (5) aggregate and record result
phase_5.alfs_store: the canonical reference: Sendov's conjecture statement and the mathematical definition of the Sendov disk |z| ≤ 1 + max|roots|; the bootstrap store for all verification logic

phase_6.cycle_cost: algebraic operations: root-finding (O(n) or higher), differentiation (O(n)), distance computations (O(n²) for all critical-point-to-root pairs), verification (O(n²))
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: uninitialized polynomial P; no roots, no critical points, no verification state
phase_6.post_cycle_state: polynomial P with computed roots, computed critical points, and a permanent verification record (PASS/FAIL/BOUNDARY); the system state is enriched with mathematical truth
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the verification cycle is entropy-conservative: information is neither created nor destroyed, only transformed and recorded. The polynomial's structure is invariant; the Sendov disk is deterministic; the verification result is a logical consequence of the input, not a random outcome. The append-only log preserves all information without loss.

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → TANCH → AFWD → CLINK → IMSCRIB → FSPLIT → EVALT → EVALF → ENGAGR → FFUSE → AREV → CLINK → IFIX
-- Class: Sendov's Theorem
-- Fingerprint: sig=(7,2,3,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=13
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(5, 9)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑡  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑼 → 𐑾  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [2] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑡 → 𐑱  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [3] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑾 → 𐑠  | composition — regime coherence
--   [4] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑱 → 𐑚  | identity — self-imscription
--   [5] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [6] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [7] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [8] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑚 → 𐑙  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [9] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑗  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [10] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑙 → 𐑱  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [11] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑗 → 𐑭  | composition — regime coherence
--   [12] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑱 → 𐑼  | irreversible fixation — winding number

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def sendovs_theorem_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑭 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑡)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (uninitialized polynomial P(z) of degree n ≥ 1; void state before any structur...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [1] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (establish the Sendov disk boundary: |z| ≤ 1 + max|r₁, r₂, ..., rₙ| where rᵢ a...)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑡 𐑱)  -- [2] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (compute the derivative P'(z); forward morphism from polynomial to its derived...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑾 𐑠)  -- [3] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (chain the operations: (1) find all roots r₁, ..., rₙ of P, (2) find all criti...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑱 𐑚)  -- [4] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (verify identity: the polynomial P is the object under examination; self-recog...)
  -- FSPLIT [5] (gran := 𐑚) (partition critical points into two branches: T-branch = {c : ∃r, |c - r| ≤ 1}, F-branch = {c : ∀r, |c - r| > 1}) / FFUSE [9] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (1 nodes)
      (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (T-branch evaluation: for each critical point in T-branch, affirm that it sati...)
      -- F-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (F-branch evaluation: for each critical point in F-branch, negate the Sendov c...)
        (.arrow 𐑳 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [8] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (boundary case: if any critical point c satisfies |c - r| = 1 for some root r,...))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [9]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑗)  -- [9] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (reconstitute the full set of critical points from both branches; verify that FFUSE(FSPLIT(critical_points)) = critical_points)
  (.arrow 𐑗 𐑙 𐑱)  -- [10] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (reverse morphism: trace critical points back to their relationship with roots...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑗 𐑭)  -- [11] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (compose the verification: (1) check all critical points against all roots, (2...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑱 𐑼)  -- [12] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (permanent record: log the polynomial P, its roots, its critical points, and t...)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def sendovs_theorem_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑭 :=
  (sendovs_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def sendovs_theorem_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑭 :=
  (sendovs_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem sendovs_theorem_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
